The card's effect is passive and slow, placing a counter during your Standby Phase. It does not initiate main combos (not a Starter), disrupt the opponent from hand (not a Handtrap), extend plays after a combo has begun (not an Extender), or clear established threats (not a Board Breaker). It also isn't a "necessary brick" that serves only to be drawn for another card's effect (not a Garnet), as it has an effect on its own.

This card is a highly specific support piece for an A-Counter strategy, accumulating and distributing A-Counters based on other card effects. It does not initiate main combos (not a Starter), disrupt the opponent from the hand (not a Handtrap), directly extend plays by enabling more summons or searches (not an Extender in the typical sense), or remove established threats on the opponent's field (not a Board Breaker). It is also not inherently undesirable to draw as a Garnet, but rather a utility card for a niche mechanic. Its role is too specific to fit the provided broad categories.

The card's primary effect sends an "Alien" monster from the Deck to the Graveyard, which serves as a setup for other "Alien" monster effects or enables future plays. Its secondary effect, activated from the Graveyard, provides a delayed search for another "Alien" monster, allowing the player to extend their resources and continue their plays in subsequent turns after an initial strategy has been established.

This card requires an "Alien" monster already on your field to activate, preventing it from being a starter. It modifies the game state by destroying one of your monsters to place A-Counters on your opponent's monsters, which sets up further plays and interactions with other "Alien" cards (e.g., those that leverage A-Counters or react to monster destruction), thereby extending your overall strategy rather than initiating it or directly breaking an opponent's board.

If this card is Special Summoned: You can target 1 Level 4 or lower Psychic monster in your GY or banishment; Special Summon it, then you can increase its Level by 1, also you cannot Special Summon from the Extra Deck for the rest of this turn, except Psychic monsters. You can only use this effect of "Silent Psychic Magician" once per turn. If this card you control would be used as Synchro Material, you can treat it as a non-Tuner.

justification: This card's primary role is to initiate the "Dracotail" deck's main Fusion Summoning strategy. Its Quick Effect allows it to begin a core play by using itself and another monster from the hand or field. Upon a successful summon, its second effect immediately generates significant advantage by setting a themed Spell/Trap and negating an opponent's monster, making it a powerful and ideal opening play.
card_text: During the Main Phase (Quick Effect): You can Fusion Summon 1 "Dracotail" Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck, using monsters from your hand or field, also you cannot Special Summon from the Extra Deck for the rest of this turn, except Fusion Monsters. If this card is sent to the GY as material for a Fusion Summon: You can Set 1 "Dracotail" Spell/Trap from your Deck, then you can negate the effects of 1 face-up monster your opponent controls. You can only use each effect of "Dracotail Mululu" once per turn.

justification: This card's effects are primarily defensive, offering protection from targeting and negating attacks for specific monsters you already control. It does not start your plays, extend your combos, break the opponent's board, or act as a handtrap. Since its role is to support an established field rather than fitting into any of the proactive or reactive categories provided, it does not match any of the given classifications.
card_text: Your opponent cannot target Rock monsters you control with 0 original ATK, with card effects. Once per battle, when an opponent's monster declares an attack: You can negate that attack, and if you do, place 1 counter on this card. If this card has 3 counters, send it to the GY. If you have 3 or more Rock monsters in your GY with 0 ATK and different names from each other: You can banish this card from your GY; any effect damage your opponent takes this turn is doubled.

justification: This is a Synchro monster that requires other monsters on the field to be summoned. Its on-summon effect to destroy a card is not a starting play but a continuation, using your established board to remove an opponent's card and extend your plays by clearing a threat.
card_text:
1 Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters
If this card is Synchro Summoned: You can target 1 card on the field; destroy it. If this card on the field is destroyed: You can target 1 face-up monster on the field; that monster cannot attack until the end of your opponent's turn. You can only use each effect of "Kuibelt the Blade Dragon" once per turn.

justification: Ken the Warrior Dragon's primary role is to be Special Summoned from the Deck by the effect of "Gen the Diamond Tiger" to enable further plays (like an Xyz Summon). Drawing "Ken the Warrior Dragon" is suboptimal, as it prevents this primary combo line and its own on-field effect is not advantageous. Therefore, it is a card required for an engine to function but is undesirable to have in the opening hand, fitting the definition of a Garnet.
card_text: Once per turn, at the end of the Battle Phase: Return this card from the field to the hand. You can only use each of the following effects of "Ken the Warrior Dragon" once per turn. During your Main Phase: You can Special Summon 1 "Gen the Diamond Tiger" from your hand or Deck to your opponent's field in Defense Position. If this card is Special Summoned by the effect of "Gen the Diamond Tiger": Your opponent draws 2 cards, then they discard 1 card.

justification: The card's primary monster effect is to Special Summon itself from the hand, but this is conditional on an Xyz Monster already being on the field. This means it cannot start a line of play by itself, but instead serves to continue or extend a combo after an Xyz Monster has been summoned.
card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
You can target 1 "Performage" Xyz Monster you control; attach this card to it as material. If a card(s) is added to your Extra Deck (except during the Damage Step): You can add 1 face-up "Performage" Pendulum Monster from your Extra Deck to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Performage Cup Tricker" once per turn.

[ Monster Effect ] 
If this card is in your hand: You can target 1 Xyz Monster on the field; detach 1 material from it, and if you do, Special Summon this card, then make 1 Xyz Monster on the field lose 600 ATK. If this card is detached from an Xyz Monster and sent to the GY to activate that monster's effect: You can target 2 Xyz Monsters you control; attach 1 material from 1 of those monsters to the other. You can only use each effect of "Performage Cup Tricker" once per turn.

justification: This card can be activated from the hand without any prior field setup. Its effect to Special Summon a Level 1 "Purrely" monster directly from the Deck is a primary way to begin the Purrely archetype's main combo sequence, making it an ideal opening card.
card_text:
The next battle or effect damage you take this turn will become 0. You can also immediately apply the following effect.
● Discard 1 card, and if you do, Special Summon 1 Level 1 "Purrely" monster from your Deck.
A "Purrely" Xyz Monster that has this card as material gains the following effect.
● Once per turn, during your opponent's Standby Phase: You can draw 1 card.

justification: This card's primary effect allows you to search for or Special Summon a key combo piece ("Diabell" monster) directly from your Deck. This requires no prior setup, making it a perfect opening card to begin your main plays.
card_text: Add 1 Level 8 or higher "Diabell" monster from your Deck to your hand, or if only your opponent controls a monster, you can Special Summon it instead. If this card is banished: You can make all "Diabell" monsters you currently control gain 500 ATK. You can only use each effect of "Filia Diabell" once per turn.

justification: This card can be discarded from the hand to search for its archetype's Field Spell ("Ryu-Ge Realm - Sea Spires"). This is a self-sufficient opening move that initiates the deck's main line of play, fitting the definition of a Starter. The effect also sets up its own graveyard effect for a future Special Summon, further solidifying its role as a combo initiator.
card_text: You can discard this card; add 1 "Ryu-Ge Realm - Sea Spires" from your Deck to your hand. During your Main Phase, if 2 or more monsters were sent from the hand and/or Deck to the GY this turn, except "Kairo Ryu-Ge Emva": You can Special Summon this card from your GY. You can place 1 "Ryu-Ge Realm - Sea Spires" you control on the bottom of the Deck; banish your opponent's entire hand, and if you do, they draw the same number of cards. You can only use each effect of "Kairo Ryu-Ge Emva" once per turn.

justification: This card's primary effect to Fusion Summon requires existing monsters in the hand or on the field, meaning a play must have already started. Its secondary effect activates from the Graveyard to destroy a monster you control and search another card, which serves as a follow-up or continuation of your combo line, fitting the definition of an Extender.
card_text: During the Main Phase: Fusion Summon 1 Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck, using monsters from your hand or field as material, including a "Memento" monster. If a monster(s) in your possession was destroyed by card effect this turn, you can also shuffle "Memento" monsters from your GY into the Deck as material. During your Main Phase: You can banish this card from your GY; destroy 1 monster you control, and if you do, add 1 "Memento" Spell/Trap from your Deck to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Mementotlan Fusion" once per turn.

justification: This card's main effect Special Summons itself as a monster and then searches an "Argostars" card from the deck. This sequence of establishing a body on the field and adding a key combo piece to the hand is the primary function of a Starter. While as a Trap it is slower than a typical monster starter, its purpose is to initiate the deck's main line of play.
card_text:
While this card is face-up in the Spell & Trap Zone, Warrior monsters you control gain 500 ATK/DEF. Once per turn: You can discard 1 card; Special Summon this card as an Effect Monster (Warrior/LIGHT/Level 4/ATK 2000/DEF 2000) with the following effect (this card is also still a Trap), then you can add 1 "Argostars" card from your Deck to your hand, except "Argostars - Lightning Tydeu".
● Once per turn (Quick Effect): You can place this card face-up in your Spell & Trap Zone.

justification: This card's primary effect tributes one monster to Special Summon a "Goblin" monster directly from the Deck. This is the main way the "Goblin Biker" deck initiates its core combos, by converting any monster on the field (like your Normal Summon) into the specific "Goblin" monster needed to begin their line of play. While it requires a monster on the field, its role is to begin the archetype's main engine, classifying it as a Starter.
card_text: Tribute 1 monster; Special Summon 1 "Goblin" monster from your Deck, but it cannot attack this turn. When a monster declares an attack: You can banish this card from your GY and detach any number of materials from "Goblin" Xyz Monsters you control; all monsters your opponent currently controls lose 1000 ATK for each material detached, until the end of this turn. You can only use each effect of "Goblin Biker Grand Breakout" once per turn.

justification: This card can be easily Normal Summoned, and its effect upon being summoned sends a "K9" card from the Deck to the GY. This "Foolish Burial" effect is a powerful way to begin a deck's main line of play by setting up the Graveyard, making it an ideal starter card. Its ability to Special Summon itself from the hand also allows it to function as an extender, but its primary role is to initiate combos.
card_text: If your opponent has 2 or more cards in their hand, you can Normal Summon this card without Tributing. You can only use each of the following effects of "K9-17 Izuna" once per turn. During the Main Phase, if your opponent has activated a monster effect in the hand or GY this turn (Quick Effect): You can Special Summon this card from your hand. If this card is Normal or Special Summoned: You can send 1 "K9" card from your Deck to the GY, except "K9-17 Izuna".

justification: This card's effects are dependent on having already started your plays by Special Summoning "Yubel" monsters to accumulate Chronicle Counters. It cannot initiate a combo on its own. Its role is to provide a toolbox of powerful follow-up effects—like reviving a monster, searching "Super Polymerization," or destroying a card—that extend and enhance an already established board state.
card_text:
Each time a monster(s) is Special Summoned that is a "Yubel" monster or mentions "Yubel", place 1 Chronicle Counter on this card. You can remove Chronicle Counters from your field to activate 1 of these effects;
● 1: Special Summon 1 "Yubel" from your GY.
● 2: Add 1 of your banished cards to your hand.
● 3: Banish 1 card from your Deck.
● 4: Destroy 1 card on the field.
● 5: Add 1 "Super Polymerization" from your Deck to your hand.
You can only use this effect of "Mature Chronicle" once per turn.

justification: This card's primary effect is activated reactively to remove a threat from the opponent's field. When an opponent's monster attacks, this trap removes that monster from the field, directly answering an established threat and breaking the opponent's battle plan.
card_text: When an opponent's monster declares an attack: Special Summon 1 Illusion monster from your hand or GY, and if you do, return the attacking monster to the hand. If a face-up Illusion monster(s) you control leaves the field by an opponent's card effect, while this card is in your GY (except during the Damage Step): You can banish this card; Special Summon 1 Illusion monster from your hand or GY. You can only use this effect of "Mirage Mirror Force" once per turn.

justification: The card's effect requires you to already have a "Ryzeal" monster and a Rank 4 Xyz monster on the field to gain its full value. Its purpose is not to start a combo, but to enhance an existing board by providing an ATK boost and an extra Xyz material. This allows for further plays after your main combo has started, fitting the definition of an Extender.
card_text:
Apply the following effect, then you can attach this card to a Rank 4 Xyz Monster you control.
● For the rest of this turn, all "Ryzeal" monsters you control gain 1000 ATK.
If this card is sent to the GY, except from the field: You can target 1 card in your opponent's GY; banish it. You can only use each effect of "Ryzeal Mass Driver" once per turn.

card_name: Anarchist Monk of the Six Samurai
justification: This card's primary effect allows it to be Special Summoned from the hand if you already control another "Six Samurai" monster. This activation condition means it cannot start a play on its own but serves to extend an existing board presence, enabling further Synchro or Link Summons and continuing your combo.
card_text:
If you control a "Six Samurai" monster other than "Anarchist Monk of the Six Samurai", you can Special Summon this card (from your hand). You can only Special Summon "Anarchist Monk of the Six Samurai" once per turn this way. You can only use each of the following effects of "Anarchist Monk of the Six Samurai" once per turn. If this card is sent from the field to the GY: You can add 1 "Six Samurai" Quick-Play Spell from your Deck to your hand. A "Six Samurai" monster that was Synchro Summoned using this card gains this effect.
● The Levels of all monsters your opponent controls are reduced by 1.

justification: This card’s effects serve to continue or extend plays rather than starting them. Its first effect requires another FIRE Warrior monster in hand to Special Summon, and its second effect requires a FIRE Warrior monster already in the Graveyard to revive. Since it relies on having other specific resources available in hand or GY, its primary role is to extend combos that are already in progress.
card_text: You can banish this card from your hand or GY; Special Summon 1 Level 4 or lower FIRE Warrior monster from your hand as a Tuner. If this card is Normal or Special Summoned: You can target 1 Level 4 or lower FIRE Warrior monster in your GY, except "Infernoble Knight Ricciardetto"; Special Summon it, also you cannot Special Summon monsters for the rest of this turn, except Warrior monsters. You can only use 1 "Infernoble Knight Ricciardetto" effect per turn, and only once that turn.

justification: This card's primary effect to get onto the field is its ability to Special Summon itself from the hand, which requires you to already control a FIRE Dragon monster. This condition means it cannot start your plays on its own but is used to continue a combo that has already begun, fitting the definition of an Extender. Its other effects, which trigger during the Battle Phase, also serve to further extend your plays into a Synchro Summon.
card_text: Once per turn, during the Battle Phase, you can (Quick Effect): Immediately after this effect resolves, Synchro Summon using this card you control. You can only use each of the following effects of "Tenpai Dragon Chundra" once per turn. If you control a FIRE Dragon monster: You can Special Summon this card from your hand. At the start of the Damage Step, if a monster battles: You can Special Summon 1 Level 4 or lower FIRE Dragon monster from your Deck, except "Tenpai Dragon Chundra".

card_name: Gaia Blaze, the Force of the Sun
justification: This card's effects are entirely focused on combat and are used to dismantle an opponent's established field. Its primary ability to attack a second time after destroying a monster by battle allows it to remove multiple threats. This role of proactively clearing the board through battle makes it a classic Board Breaker.
card_text:
1 Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters
If this attacking card destroys an opponent's monster by battle: It can make a second attack in a row. Once per turn, at the end of the Battle Phase: You can target FIRE monsters in your GY up to the number of monsters this card destroyed by battle this turn; add them to your hand.

justification: This card can be activated without any prior setup to send a key Pyro monster from the Deck to the GY. This effect is a primary way to initiate combos for graveyard-centric Pyro decks (like "Volcanic"), making it an ideal card to open with to start your main line of play.
card_text:
Send 1 Pyro monster from your Deck to the GY, then, if you sent a "Volcanic" monster to the GY, you can apply 1 of these effects.
● Inflict damage to your opponent equal to its Level x 100.
● Special Summon 1 "Bomb Token" (Pyro/FIRE/Level 1/ATK 1000/DEF 1000) to your opponent's field. Each time 1 is destroyed, its controller takes 500 damage.
You can only activate 1 "Fire Ejection" per turn.

justification: The card's first effect Special Summons itself from the hand only after another card has been destroyed, meaning a play has already occurred. Its primary combo effect requires it to already be on the field, using another Dinosaur monster you control or have in hand to further your plays. This role of continuing a sequence after an initial move has been made fits the definition of an Extender.
card_text: If a card(s) is destroyed by battle or card effect: You can Special Summon this card from your hand. During your Main Phase: You can destroy 1 Dinosaur monster in your hand or field, then Special Summon 1 Dinosaur Normal Monster from your hand or Deck, also you cannot Special Summon monsters from the Extra Deck for the rest of this turn, except Dragon, Dinosaur, Sea Serpent, or Wyrm monsters. You can only use each effect of "Xeno Meteorus" once per turn.

justification: This card is summoned from the Extra Deck, requiring prior setup (having its materials available). Its first effect Special Summons an Illusion monster from the Graveyard, which directly continues and extends your plays by providing another monster on the field for further combo lines. This fits the definition of an extender, as it builds upon an existing board state to generate more value.
card_text:
1 Illusion monster + 1 Level 6 or higher Fiend monster
You can target 1 Illusion monster in your GY, except "Azamina Moa Regina"; Special Summon it. When an "Azamina" or "Sinful Spoils" card or effect is activated, except "Azamina Moa Regina" (Quick Effect): You can target up to 2 cards on the field; destroy them. You can only use each effect of "Azamina Moa Regina" once per turn.

justification: This card can be Normal Summoned to immediately activate its effect, which sets a key combo Spell/Trap ("Evolutionary Bridge" or "Evo-Singularity") from the Deck. This ability to single-handedly initiate the deck's main line of play without needing any prior board presence makes it a quintessential Starter.
card_text: If this card is Normal or Special Summoned: You can Set 1 "Evolutionary Bridge" or "Evo-Singularity" directly from your Deck. During your Main Phase, if you control this card that was Normal Summoned, or Special Summoned by the effect of a FIRE monster: You can send 1 FIRE Reptile or Dinosaur monster from your Deck to the GY, then you can make 2 monsters on the field become the same Type and Level as that monster sent to the GY. You can only use each effect of "Evolsaur Lios" once per turn.

justification: The card's main effect Special Summons itself from the hand or GY, but only after another Fiend monster is sent to the GY by a card effect. This condition requires a play to have already been initiated, and this card's summon serves to continue or extend that line of play for further combos.
card_text: If a Fiend monster(s), except "Diabolica the Draconique General", is sent to your GY by card effect (except during the Damage Step): You can Special Summon this card from your GY (if it was there when the monster was sent) or hand (even if not), but banish it when it leaves the field. If this card is sent to the GY by card effect: You can target 1 Fiend monster in your GY, except "Diabolica the Draconique General"; add it to your hand. You can only use 1 "Diabolica the Draconique General" effect per turn, and only once that turn.

justification: This card's Special Summon condition triggers only when it's added to the hand by an effect (not drawn), requiring another card to initiate its presence on the field. Additionally, its ability to count as two Xyz materials for specific summons helps continue and extend your plays, facilitating the summon of stronger monsters after a combo has started.
card_text:
A "Shark Drake" Xyz Monster that has this card as material gains this effect.
● Once per turn: You can detach 2 materials from this card, then target 1 Spell/Trap on the field; attach it to this card as material.
You can only use each of the following effects of "Drake Shark" once per turn. If this card is added to your hand, except by drawing it: You can Special Summon this card. This card can be treated as 2 materials for the Xyz Summon of a WATER Xyz Monster that requires 3 or more materials.

justification: Once Fusion Summoned, this card's Quick Effect allows it to return multiple face-up cards your opponent controls to their hand. This effect directly neutralizes or eliminates established threats on the opponent's field, functioning as a powerful form of disruption and board clearing, which is a key characteristic of a Board Breaker, especially given its Quick Effect timing.
card_text:
1 "Melodious Maestra" monster + 2 "Melodious" monsters
(Quick Effect): You can banish (until the End Phase) any number of "Melodious" monsters you control, then you can return up to that many face-up cards your opponent controls to the hand. You can only use this effect of "Flowering Etoile the Melodious Magnificat" once per turn. If this face-up Fusion Summoned card in its owner's control leaves the field because of an opponent's card: You can Special Summon 1 "Melodious" monster from your Deck or Extra Deck, except "Flowering Etoile the Melodious Magnificat".

justification: Upon Normal or Special Summon, this card can immediately Special Summon another Psychic monster from the Deck, allowing it to begin a main line of play and make significant progress towards establishing a board and setting up further combos, even by itself as a Normal Summon.
card_text: If this card is Normal or Special Summoned: You can banish 1 card from your hand, and if you do, Special Summon 1 Level 4 or lower Psychic monster from your Deck, except "Serene Psychic Girl", also you cannot Special Summon from the Extra Deck for the rest of this turn, except Psychic monsters. You can target face-up monsters on the field up to the number of Psychic monsters you control; increase their Levels by 1 until the end of this turn. You can only use each effect of "Serene Psychic Girl" once per turn.

justification: This card is a Synchro Monster, requiring specific materials to be summoned. Its effects, particularly drawing a card and destroying an opponent's monster upon Special Summon, provide card advantage and remove threats, allowing the player to continue or optimize their plays after the initial Synchro Summoning setup has occurred. It helps push through interruptions or get more value after the main combo starts, rather than initiating it.
card_text:
1 Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters
Your monsters with 2000 or less ATK cannot be destroyed by battle. You can only use each of the following effects of "Centur-Ion Legatia" once per turn. If this card is Special Summoned: You can draw 1 card, then you can destroy the monster your opponent controls with the highest ATK (your choice, if tied). During the End Phase: You can place 1 non-Synchro "Centur-Ion" monster from your hand or GY in your Spell & Trap Zone as a face-up Continuous Trap.

justification: As an Extra Deck monster, this card is not a starter. Its primary effect activates upon being Synchro Summoned, which means a play has already begun. It then Special Summons additional monsters to continue or extend the current combo, perfectly fitting the role of an extender by building more board presence or enabling further summons. Its revival effect also provides follow-up or recovery to extend plays.
card_text:
1 WATER Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters
If this card is Synchro Summoned: You can Special Summon 1 Level 4 or lower Fish monster from your hand or GY in Defense Position, then you can Special Summon 1 monster with that same name from your GY. You can only use this effect of "White Aura Porpoise" once per turn. If this card in your possession is destroyed by your opponent's card and sent to your GY: You can banish 1 other WATER monster from your GY; Special Summon this card, and if you do, it is treated as a Tuner.

justification: This card can be activated during your Main Phase to directly place a "Millennium" monster from your Deck into your Spell & Trap Zone, effectively searching a key piece of your archetype's strategy. This allows you to begin your main combo or line of play by assembling necessary components, making it a powerful opener for the "Millennium" theme.
card_text: During your Main Phase: You can place 1 monster from your hand in your Spell & Trap Zone as a face-up Continuous Spell, then place 1 "Millennium" monster from your Deck in your Spell & Trap Zone as a face-up Continuous Spell. You can only use this effect of "Wedju Temple" once per turn. If a face-up "Millennium" monster(s) you control is destroyed by battle or card effect, you can place it in your Spell & Trap Zone as a face-up Continuous Spell, instead of sending it to the GY.

justification: This card requires 10 prior card activations on the field to fully activate its main effect, fitting the criteria of needing "something to have started already." Once its condition is met, it sets a Continuous Trap from the Deck that can Special Summon itself as a monster, providing "more value" or "extending" the player's board and plays after significant setup.
card_text: Each time a card or effect is activated, except "That's 10!": You can place 1 Access Counter on this card (max. 10). Monsters you control gain 100 ATK for each Access Counter on this card. If the 10th Access Counter is placed on this card: You can return this card to the hand, and if you do, Set from your Deck, 1 Continuous Trap with an effect that Special Summons itself as a monster. It can be activated this turn.

justification: The card's effect activates in response to cards being sent from hand or Deck to the GY, a common action during a player's own combo. By recycling a card from the GY back into the Deck, "Sweet Roommaid" enables the player to recover crucial resources, allowing them to continue or extend their plays or gain more value after a primary action has occurred, fitting the role of an extender.
card_text: If a card(s) is sent from the hand and/or Deck to the GY: Target 1 card in either GY; shuffle it into the Deck. You can only activate 1 "Sweet Roommaid" per turn.

justification: This card is a reactive Trap that activates when damage is taken from the opponent. Its effects provide either defensive recovery (Special Summon and end Battle Phase) or counter-damage (burn). It does not initiate main combos (not a Starter), cannot be activated from the hand (not a Handtrap), does not extend one's own plays (not an Extender), and does not directly remove or neutralize established threats on the opponent's field (not a Board Breaker). It is also not a necessary but undesirable draw for a combo (not a Garnet). Therefore, it does not fit the provided categories.
card_text:
When you take damage from your opponent's card effect or their monster's attack: Apply the appropriate effect based on the type of damage.
● Battle: Special Summon 1 monster from your GY, with ATK less than or equal to the damage, and if you do, end the Battle Phase after the Damage Step.
● Effect: Inflict double the damage you took to your opponent.
You can only activate 1 "Split Mirror of the Underworld" per turn.

justification: This card is an Extra Deck monster that requires specific Ritual Beast materials to be summoned. Its Quick Effect allows it to return itself to the Extra Deck and Special Summon a banished Ritual Beast monster, which is a core "tag-out" mechanic used by Ritual Beast decks to extend combos, dodge interruptions, or recover resources after an initial play has been made.
card_text:
1 "Ritual Beast Tamer" monster + 1 "Spiritual Beast" monster
Must be Special Summoned (from your Extra Deck) by banishing the above cards from your field and/or GY. You can only Special Summon "Ritual Beast Ulti-Nochiudrago(s)" once per turn. Your opponent cannot target other "Ritual Beast" monsters you control with card effects. (Quick Effect): You can return this card to the Extra Deck, then target 1 of your banished "Ritual Beast" monsters; Special Summon it, but it cannot attack directly.

justification: The card's primary activated effect allows Special Summoning "Flame Swordsman" from the Extra Deck by sending a monster from the hand or field to the GY. This requires an existing resource (a monster to send) to further develop the board or combo, fitting the definition of an Extender that continues or extends plays after something has already started. The other effects (Normal Summon protection and battle trick) are supportive but do not initiate a main combo.
card_text: Your opponent cannot activate cards or effects when you Normal Summon a FIRE Warrior monster. Once per turn: You can send 1 monster from your hand or face-up field to the GY; Special Summon 1 "Flame Swordsman" from your Extra Deck. (This is treated as a Fusion Summon.) Once per turn, when a monster declares an attack: You can target 1 Warrior monster you control; until the end of this turn, it loses exactly 1000 ATK, but all other monsters you currently control gain 1000 ATK.

justification: This card requires another Zombie monster to already be on the field to Special Summon itself from the hand, fitting the definition of an extender that continues or extends plays after something has already started. Its second effect also facilitates further Special Summons, pushing combo progression.
card_text: If you control a Zombie monster: You can Special Summon this card from your hand. During your Main Phase: You can return this card from the field to the hand, then you can Special Summon 1 Zombie monster from your hand. You can only use each effect of "Vulcarrion the Rotting Phoenix" once per turn.

justification: This card functions as a primary enabler for the "Horus" archetype. Its Main Phase effect searches any "Horus" monster from the Deck, allowing the player to immediately access key combo pieces and begin their main line of play, making it an ideal card to open with for establishing a board.
card_text: This card's name becomes "King's Sarcophagus" while in the Field Zone. During your Main Phase: You can add 1 "Horus" monster from your Deck to your hand, then place 1 card from your hand on the bottom of the Deck. You can only use this effect of "Walls of the Imperial Tomb" once per turn. If you activate the effect of "Horus the Black Flame Deity": You can draw 1 card.

justification: The card's primary strategic role is to establish a strong board by directly placing two key "Argostars" Continuous Traps from the Deck onto the field. This effect, accessible after a Normal Summon, allows the player to begin their main line of play and make significant progress towards their deck's win condition right from the start, fitting the definition of a Starter.
card_text: During your opponent's turn, if you control a Continuous Trap Card (Quick Effect): You can banish this card from your hand; make the ATK of 1 monster on the field 0 until the end of this turn. You can target 1 "Dracoslayer" or "Dinomist" card in your other Pendulum Zone; Special Summon it. You can only use this effect of "Dinomight Powerload, the Dracoslayer" once per turn.

[ Monster Effect ] 
(This card is always treated as a "Dinomist" card.)
If this card is Tributed: You can add 1 face-up "Dracoslayer" or "Dinomist" Pendulum Monster from your Extra Deck to your hand, except "Dinomight Powerload, the Dracoslayer". You can only use this effect of "Dinomight Powerload, the Dracoslayer" once per turn.

justification: As an Xyz Monster, "Kashtira Shangri-Ira" requires 2+ Level 7 monsters to be on the field for its Summon, meaning plays must have already begun. Once summoned, its effects allow for further Special Summons from the Deck and zone manipulation, which significantly extend and solidify the player's board and control after the initial setup.
card_text:
2+ Level 7 monsters
During each Standby Phase: You can Special Summon 1 "Kashtira" monster from your Deck. You can only use the previous effect of "Kashtira Shangri-Ira" once per turn. Each time a card(s) your opponent owns and possesses is banished face-down (except during the Damage Step): You can choose 1 unused Main Monster Zone or Spell & Trap Zone; it cannot be used while this monster is face-up on the field. If this card on the field would be destroyed by battle or card effect, you can detach 1 material from this card instead.

card_name: On Your Mark, Get Set, DUEL!
justification: The card's primary effect allows it to search a "Synchron" monster from the Deck if no other cards are controlled, making it an excellent card to open with to immediately begin a Synchro combo or line of play.
card_text: When this card is activated: If you control no other cards, you can add 1 "Synchron" monster from your Deck to your hand. Once per turn, during your Standby Phase: Place 1 Signal Counter on this card. You can remove 2 Signal Counters from your field and send this face-up card to the GY; draw 2 cards, then send 1 card from your hand to the GY. You can only activate 1 "On Your Mark, Get Set, DUEL!" per turn.

justification: This card can be sent from the hand to Special Summon an "Elemental HERO" or "Neo-Spacian" monster from the hand or GY. This allows a player to establish a monster on the field without consuming their Normal Summon, effectively initiating or significantly advancing their main combo line, making it a strong opening play.
card_text: You can send this card from your hand or field to the GY; Special Summon 1 "Elemental HERO" monster or 1 "Neo-Spacian" monster from your hand or GY, but negate its effects if Summoned from the GY. If you Special Summon an "Elemental HERO" Fusion Monster, while this card is in your GY (except during the Damage Step): You can banish this card; draw 2 cards, then place 1 card from your hand on the bottom of the Deck. You can only use each effect of "Cross Keeper" once per turn.

justification: The primary effect allows the player to negate a face-up monster's effects, which is a direct way to neutralize an established threat on the opponent's field and is especially useful when going second to clear their setup. The subsequent effect to set a Normal Trap further enables plays after dealing with the opponent's board.
card_text: Target 1 face-up monster on the field; its controller's opponent takes damage equal to the number of cards in their hand x 200, then negate that target's effects until the end of this turn. If effect damage is inflicted (except during the Damage Step): You can banish this card from your GY; Set 1 Normal Trap directly from your hand or Deck. If Set from the hand, it can be activated this turn. You can only use 1 "Angel of Blue Tears" effect per turn, and only once that turn.

justification: The first effect generates a Token when the card leaves the Monster Zone, allowing for continued field presence and further summons after it's used as material. The second effect, activated from the GY, requires a Token to be present (indicating plays have already begun) and provides additional bodies, extending the board even with a restriction on further Special Summons. Both effects facilitate continued plays rather than initiating them.
card_text: If this card is sent from the Monster Zone to the GY: You can Special Summon 1 "Tellus Wing Token" (Fairy/LIGHT/Level 1/ATK 0/DEF 0). While you control a "Tellus Wing Token": You can banish this card from your GY and 1 Spell from your hand; Special Summon 2 "Tellus Wing Tokens", also you cannot Special Summon for the rest of this turn, except from your hand. You can only use each effect of "Tellus the Little Angel" once per turn.

justification: The second effect allows for the Special Summon of "Kuribabylon" by tributing itself and four other specific "Kuriboh" monsters from your hand and/or field. This requires an extensive setup of resources (something to have started already, specifically the accumulation of these particular monsters) and then converts those resources into a stronger monster, thereby extending your plays. The first effect also provides limited extension by replacing a battle-destroyed "Kuriboh" monster.
card_text:
(This card is always treated as a "Kuriboh" card.)
When this card, or another "Kuriboh" monster, you control is destroyed by battle: You can Special Summon 1 monster with 300 ATK/200 DEF from your Deck, except "Kuribah". You can only use this effect of "Kuribah" once per turn. You can Tribute this card you control and 1 each of "Kuribee", "Kuriboo", "Kuribeh" and "Kuriboh" from your hand and/or field; Special Summon 1 "Kuribabylon" from your hand, Deck, or GY.

justification: This card is an Extra Deck monster that requires two Level 4 monsters to be summoned, meaning it cannot be the initial card played from hand. Once on the field, its effects to search/mill "Photon" or "Galaxy" cards and modify the Levels of Special Summoned LIGHT monsters directly enable further Xyz Summons or combo progressions, fitting the role of a card that continues or extends plays after an initial setup has begun.
card_text:
2 Level 4 monsters
Other LIGHT monsters you control gain 500 ATK. You can only use each of the following effects of "Galaxy Photon Dragon" once per turn. You can detach 1 material from this card; either add 1 "Photon" or "Galaxy" card from your Deck to your hand, or send it to the GY. If a LIGHT monster(s) is Special Summoned to your field (except during the Damage Step): You can target 1 of those monsters; its Level becomes 4 or 8 until the end of this turn.

justification: The card's Monster Effect allows it to search for "Supreme King Dragon," "Supreme King Gate," or "Soul of the Supreme King" cards after inflicting battle damage, providing additional resources to continue or extend combo plays after it has been summoned and attacked. Its Pendulum Effect also offers defensive utility and sets up banished Spells, which can further enable future plays. These effects require the card to be on the field (or in the Pendulum Zone) and often rely on prior actions, fitting the definition of an extender that helps gain more value after plays have started.
card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
Once per turn, at the start of the Damage Step, if a monster battles a monster: You can return this card to the hand, and if you do, banish 1 Spell from your Deck. For that battle, your monster cannot be destroyed, also the battle damage you take is halved.

[ Monster Effect ] 
When this card inflicts battle damage to your opponent: You can add 1 "Supreme King Dragon" monster, "Supreme King Gate" monster, or "Soul of the Supreme King" from your Deck to your hand. Once per turn, during the Standby Phase: Activate this effect; each player can banish 1 Spell from their Deck. Once per turn, when a monster declares an attack: Activate this effect; the attacked player can apply the following effect.
● Add 1 of your banished Spells to your hand, then discard it, and if you do, negate that attack.

justification: This card's primary effect is to facilitate the Ritual Summoning of "Prediction Princess" Ritual Monsters, which is the core summoning mechanic and main line of play for the archetype. While it requires a Ritual Monster and Tributes in hand, the Ritual Spell itself is the action card that initiates this fundamental process, enabling the deck to begin its main strategy and make meaningful progress. Its second effect in the GY acts as an extender, but the initial role of enabling the Ritual Summon classifies it as a Starter.
card_text: This card is used to Ritual Summon any "Prediction Princess" Ritual Monster from your hand or GY in face-up Attack Position or face-down Defense Position. You must also Tribute monsters from your hand or field, whose total Levels equal or exceed the Level of the Ritual Monster you Ritual Summon. During the Standby Phase, if you control a "Prediction Princess" Ritual Monster: You can banish this card from your GY; Special Summon 1 non-Ritual "Prediction Princess" monster from your Deck in face-down Defense Position.

justification: The card's Pendulum Effect allows you to place a Pendulum Monster from the Extra Deck into a Pendulum Zone if the other zone is empty, which helps complete or restore your Pendulum Scale after a play has begun or been interrupted. Its Monster Effect also allows it to return to the Pendulum Zone upon destruction, further extending your resources and ability to make additional Pendulum Summons. Both effects serve to continue or extend plays, rather than initiate them.
card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
If you have no card in your other Pendulum Zone: You can place 1 face-up Pendulum Monster from your Extra Deck in your Pendulum Zone. You can only use this effect of "Patissciel Couverture" once per turn.

[ Monster Effect ] 
2 Pendulum Monsters
If this card in the Monster Zone is destroyed: You can place this card in your Pendulum Zone.

justification: The card's primary effect requires a "Destiny HERO" monster to already be in the GY, allowing for further Special Summons or resource recovery. The second effect also requires "Destiny HERO" monsters to be on the field. Both effects serve to continue or extend plays after the initial setup, rather than starting a combo from scratch or disrupting the opponent.
card_text: Banish 1 "Destiny HERO" monster from your GY; add to your hand or Special Summon 1 "Destiny HERO" monster from your GY. You can banish this card from your GY, then target 2 "Destiny HERO" monsters you control; choose 1 of those monsters, its ATK becomes the other monster's ATK, until the end of this turn.

justification: This card's primary effect to Special Summon a Pendulum Monster activates upon its Synchro Summon. This means it requires an existing board (a Tuner and non-Tuner) to be made, and then it continues and extends your plays by bringing out an additional monster to further combo or establish more presence.
card_text:
1 Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters
If this card is Synchro Summoned: You can Special Summon 1 Level 4 or lower Pendulum Monster from your hand or face-up from your Extra Deck. At the end of the Damage Step, when this card or your Pendulum Monster battles an opponent's monster, but the opponent's monster was not destroyed by the battle: You can destroy that opponent's monster. You can only use each effect of "Tilting Entrainment" once per turn.

justification: This card can initiate a main line of play by Special Summoning itself from the hand (with a discard cost for an easily accessible archetype/attribute) and setting a powerful Trap Card directly from the Deck, making meaningful progress towards establishing a board and enabling further combo potential or immediate disruption.
card_text: You can discard 1 other EARTH Fairy monster; Special Summon this card from your hand, then you can place 1 "Gravekeeper's Trap" from your Deck face-up in your Spell & Trap Zone. (Quick Effect): You can banish this card from your field or GY, then target up to 3 cards in any GY(s), or up to 5 if "Exchange of the Spirit" is on your field or in your GY; shuffle them into the Deck. You can only use each effect of "Mudora the Sword Oracle" once per turn.

justification: This card is a Fusion Monster, meaning it requires existing resources (materials and a Fusion Spell) to be summoned. Once on the field, its Quick Effect provides versatile options, including Special Summoning other monsters from the Deck/hand and recycling resources, which directly serve to continue or extend a player's established combo or maintain board presence, rather than initiating a play from scratch or being a standalone disruption from the hand.
card_text:
1 "Witchcrafter" monster + 1 Spellcaster monster
When a Spell Card or effect, or a non-Fusion Spellcaster monster effect, is activated (Quick Effect): You can activate 1 of these effects (but you can only use each effect of "Witchcrafter Vice-Madame" once per turn).
● Destroy 1 card on the field.
● Special Summon 1 Level 6 or lower "Witchcrafter" monster from your hand or Deck.
● Add 1 "Witchcrafter" Spell/Trap from your GY to your hand.

justification: This card is an Extra Deck monster that, upon its Fusion Summon, immediately allows for another Fusion Summon, effectively continuing and extending the ongoing combo. It builds upon an initial play rather than starting one, fitting the role of an extender that helps push through or gain more value after a main combo begins.
card_text:
"Fallen of Albaz" + 1 LIGHT monster
If this card is Fusion Summoned: You can Fusion Summon 1 Level 8 or lower Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck, except "Albion the Branded Dragon", by banishing Fusion Materials mentioned on it from your hand, field, and/or GY. During the End Phase, if this card is in the GY because it was sent there this turn: You can add to your hand or Set 1 "Branded" Spell/Trap directly from your Deck. You can only use each effect of "Albion the Branded Dragon" once per turn.

card_name: Ancient Warriors Saga - Deception and Betrayal
justification: The card's first effect allows you to Special Summon an "Ancient Warriors" monster from your hand during your Main Phase. This enables you to get a key monster onto the field without needing a pre-established board, effectively beginning your main combo or line of play.
card_text: During your Main Phase: You can Special Summon 1 "Ancient Warriors" monster from your hand, and if you do, take damage equal to its Level x 100. You can send this face-up card from the Spell & Trap Zone to the GY, then target 1 "Ancient Warriors" monster on the field; change it to any Attribute, then, if you targeted an opponent's monster, you can take control of it. You can only use each effect of "Ancient Warriors Saga - Deception and Betrayal" once per turn.

justification: This card activates from the GY, requiring a Reptile monster already on your field to be used, indicating that initial plays must have already occurred. Its effects allow for further interaction with the opponent's board (flipping down a monster) or enabling specific synergies with your own monsters (destroying your own to weaken the opponent's), thereby continuing or extending your established plays.
card_text:
You can banish this card from your GY, then target 1 Reptile monster you control and 1 face-up monster your opponent controls, to activate 1 of these effects;
● Change those monsters to face-down Defense Position.
● Destroy that monster you control, and if you do, change the ATK of the other monster to 0 until the end of this turn.
You can only use this effect of "Yamorimori" once per turn.

justification: This card requires a Fusion Summon to activate its effect, meaning another card or action (like "Branded Fusion") must have initiated the play. Once summoned, its ability to immediately Fusion Summon another monster from the Extra Deck allows the player to extend their combo and build a stronger board, fitting the role of an extender.
card_text:
1 DARK monster + "Fallen of Albaz"
If this card is Fusion Summoned: You can discard 1 card; Fusion Summon 1 Level 8 or lower Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck, except "Lubellion the Searing Dragon", by shuffling Fusion Materials mentioned on it into the Deck, from your monsters on the field, GY, and/or face-up banished cards. For the rest of this turn, this card cannot attack, also you cannot Special Summon from the Extra Deck, except Fusion Monsters. You can only use this effect of "Lubellion the Searing Dragon" once per turn.

justification: The card's primary effect on activation recovers "Amazoness" monsters from the GY or Extra Deck, allowing you to recycle resources and continue your plays after monsters have been used for summons or sent to the GY. This requires prior actions (having a monster in the GY or Extra Deck) and helps extend existing combos rather than starting them from scratch.
card_text: When this card is activated: You can take 1 "Amazoness" monster from your GY or your face-up Extra Deck, and either add it to your hand or (if it is a Pendulum Monster) place it in your Pendulum Zone. If a monster(s) is Special Summoned to your opponent's field while you control an "Amazoness" Monster Card (except during the Damage Step): You can target 1 of those opponent's monsters; gain LP equal to its ATK. You can only use this effect of "Amazoness Hall" once per turn.

justification: This card's primary effect is to return cards from the opponent's field to their hand, specifically targeting threats in a column. Its activation condition requires the opponent to have an established card, and its strategic role is to eliminate or answer those threats on the opponent's field, aligning with the definition of a Board Breaker.
card_text: Activate this card only if your opponent controls a card in this Set card's column; return all other cards in this card's column to the hand. Neither player can use the unused zones in this card's column. You can only control 1 "Sour Scheduling - Red Vinegar Vamoose".

justification: This card's Normal Summon effect requires an "Attraction" Trap to already be in the GY, meaning it relies on a prior play having occurred. Its Quick Effect further requires an "Attraction" Trap to already be equipped to a monster. Both effects serve to continue or extend the "Amazement" deck's strategy by re-using or re-positioning its "Attraction" Traps, rather than starting a combo from scratch.
card_text: When this card is Normal Summoned: You can target 1 "Attraction" Trap in your GY and 1 face-up monster your opponent controls; equip that Trap to your opponent's monster. (Quick Effect): You can target 1 of your "Attraction" Traps equipped to a monster; equip it to 1 "Amazement" monster you control or 1 face-up monster your opponent controls. You can only use this effect of "Amazement Ambassador Bufo" once per turn.

justification: This card can be Normal Summoned and then Tributed to Special Summon any other "Chronomaly" monster from the Deck, effectively acting as a one-card starter to bring out a key combo piece and begin the deck's main line of play.
card_text: You can Tribute this card; Special Summon 1 "Chronomaly" monster from your Deck, except "Chronomaly Magella Globe". If this card is in your GY, except the turn it was sent there: You can banish 1 "Chronomaly" card from your GY, except "Chronomaly Magella Globe"; add this card to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Chronomaly Magella Globe" once per turn.

justification: This card cannot initiate a main combo by itself, as it requires a Field Spell Card to be activated (either yours or the opponent's) to Special Summon itself from the hand. Once that condition is met, it provides an additional monster on the field, allowing for further Link, Xyz, or Synchro summons, thereby extending existing plays rather than starting them.
card_text: If a Field Spell Card is activated: You can Special Summon this card from your hand, then, if face-up cards are in both Field Zones, this card cannot be destroyed by battle or card effects this turn. You can only use this effect of "Meowseclick" once per turn. During your turn, the effects of the face-up card in your opponent's Field Zone are negated. During your opponent's turn, the effects of the face-up card in your Field Zone are negated.

justification: This is an Extra Deck monster that requires 2+ Insect monsters to be summoned, meaning it cannot start your plays. Its primary activated effect, tributing an Insect monster to Special Summon a "Beetrooper" from the Deck, is a classic extender effect that continues your combo after you've already established a board.
card_text:
2+ Insect monsters
While this Link Summoned card has 3000 or less ATK, your opponent cannot target this card with card effects, also it cannot be destroyed by your opponent's card effects. You cannot Special Summon monsters, except Insect monsters. You can Tribute 1 Insect monster, then activate 1 of these effects;
● Special Summon 1 "Beetrooper" monster from your Deck.
● This card gains 2000 ATK until the end of this turn.
You can only use this effect of "Giant Beetrooper Invincible Atlas" once per turn.

justification: This card primarily functions to continue or extend plays. Its first effect allows it to bring out another copy of itself upon Normal or Special Summon, providing additional Link Material, though it imposes a strict Cyberse Special Summon restriction. The second effect, which allows it to search any "G Golem" card when sent from the hand to the GY, is a powerful way to cycle resources and gain card advantage during a combo, especially when discarded as a cost for another card's effect. Both effects help push through interruptions or expand board presence after an initial play has begun, fitting the definition of an Extender.
card_text: If this card is Normal or Special Summoned: You can add to your hand or Special Summon 1 "G Golem Pebble Dog" from your Deck, also you cannot Special Summon monsters for the rest of this turn, except Cyberse monsters. If this card is sent from the hand to the GY: You can add 1 "G Golem" card from your Deck to your hand. You can only use each effect of "G Golem Pebble Dog" once per turn.

justification: This card cannot be summoned unless 3 Insect monsters are already banished, meaning previous plays must have occurred. It extends your board by bringing out a strong monster, leveraging existing resources (banished cards), and can provide resilience with its self-revival effect if destroyed or banished by the opponent.
card_text: Cannot be Normal Summoned/Set. Must first be Special Summoned (from your hand) by shuffling 3 of your banished Insect monsters into the Main Deck. During the Main Phase, if this card in its owner's control is destroyed or banished by an opponent's card effect: You can Special Summon this card. You can only use this effect of "Heavy Beetrooper Mighty Neptune" once per turn. Once per turn, during the End Phase: You can target 1 other Insect monster you control; it gains 1000 ATK.

justification: The card's Pendulum Effect requires a specific Field Spell or another "Vaylantz" monster on the field to Special Summon itself, indicating it continues plays after an initial setup. Its Monster Effects further extend combos by potentially searching for more "Vaylantz" cards or Special Summoning additional monsters from the Spell & Trap Zone, pushing for more board presence after the Vaylantz engine has begun.
card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
If "Vaylantz World - Konig Wissen" is in a Field Zone or if you control a FIRE "Vaylantz" monster: You can Special Summon this card to your Main Monster Zone in its same column. You can only use this effect of "Vaylantz Mad Marquess" once per turn.

[ Monster Effect ] 
During your Main Phase: You can roll a six-sided die, excavate that many cards from the top of your Deck (or as many as you can), and if you do, you can add 1 excavated "Vaylantz" card to your hand, also shuffle the rest into the Deck. If this card in the Monster Zone moves to another Monster Zone: You can roll a six-sided die, and if you roll a 2, 3, 4, or 5, Special Summon 1 Monster Card from your Spell & Trap Zone to your Main Monster Zone in its same column. You can only use each effect of "Vaylantz Mad Marquess" once per turn.

justification: The card's first effect, while a reactive Trap, Special Summons a monster, allowing for further Link Summons in subsequent turns, thus extending your board presence. Its second effect, activated from the GY, enables your "Marincess" Link monster to attack multiple times, directly extending your combat phase plays after your main combo (summoning a high Link Rating monster) has been established.
card_text: When a monster declares an attack: Negate the attack, and if you do, Special Summon 1 "Marincess Crystal Heart" from your Extra Deck or GY. You can banish this card from your GY, then target 1 Link-2 or higher "Marincess" monster you control; it can attack a number of times each Battle Phase this turn, up to its Link Rating, also your opponent takes no battle damage from attacks involving it this turn. You can only activate 1 "Marincess Bubble Ring" per turn.

card_name: Gate Guardian of Wind and Water
justification: This card requires its specific materials ("Kazejin" and "Suijin") to already be on the field to be Special Summoned. It does not initiate a combo but rather extends or culminates a play by converting existing monsters into a powerful negation body, thus helping to push through interruptions or enhance the established board after an initial setup.
card_text:
"Kazejin" + "Suijin"
Must be Special Summoned (from your Extra Deck) by banishing the above cards you control. Once per Chain, when your opponent activates a Spell/Trap Card or effect on the field (Quick Effect): You can negate that effect. You can only use this effect of "Gate Guardian of Wind and Water" twice per turn. If this face-up Special Summoned card in its owner's control leaves the field because of an opponent's card: You can Special Summon 1 of your banished "Kazejin" or "Suijin".

justification: As a Synchro Monster, it requires other monsters to be on the field to be summoned. Its effects activate upon Synchro Summon or when used as Synchro Material for another summon, both of which serve to advance existing plays, generate resources (banishing from Deck, searching from Deck), and extend combo lines, fitting the definition of an extender.
card_text:
1 Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters
If this card is Synchro Summoned: You can banish 1 Level 6 or lower Fish monster from your Deck. If this card is sent to the GY as Synchro Material: You can target 1 Fish monster in your GY; banish that target, then you can add 1 Fish monster with an equal or lower Level from your Deck to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Arionpos, Serpent of the Ghoti" once per turn.

justification: This card requires 2+ Reptile monsters to be Link Summoned, meaning a play must have already started to bring out its materials. Once on the field, its effect to search a card that places A-Counters and its ability to grant an additional Normal Summon for a Reptile monster directly extend ongoing combos and generate further value, fitting the role of an Extender.
card_text:
2+ Reptile monsters
Monsters your opponent controls with A-Counters are changed to Defense Position, also neither player can activate their effects. You can only use each of the following effects of "Cosmic Slicer Zer'oll" once per turn. If this card is Link Summoned: You can add 1 card from your Deck to your hand that has an effect that places A-Counter(s). You can remove 2 A-Counters from anywhere on the field; immediately after this effect resolves, Normal Summon 1 Reptile monster.

justification: This card is a Link Monster whose effect recycles other Plant Link Monsters from the Graveyard to the Extra Deck. This allows for the reuse of resources and continued Link Summons, which is highly beneficial after initial plays have been made and Link Monsters have accumulated in the GY, thus extending existing combo lines rather than starting them.
card_text:
2 Plant monsters
This card cannot be targeted for attacks, but does not prevent your opponent from attacking you directly. You can Tribute 1 monster, then target 2 Plant Link Monsters in your GY, except "Sunavalon Daphne"; return them to the Extra Deck. You can only use this effect of "Sunavalon Daphne" once per turn.

justification: While a Fusion Monster, "Tearlaments Kitkallos" is the primary enabling card for the "Tearlaments" deck's core strategy. Its effect upon Special Summon searches or mills a "Tearlaments" card, which immediately begins or significantly progresses the deck's main combo loop of recursive Fusion Summons and milling. The deck's primary goal is often to summon this card early to activate its effects and initiate the main line of play, making it the central engine and an effective starter for the deck's operations.
card_text:
1 "Tearlaments" monster + 1 Aqua monster
If this card is Special Summoned: You can take 1 "Tearlaments" card from your Deck, and either add it to your hand or send it to the GY. You can target 1 monster you control; Special Summon 1 "Tearlaments" monster from your hand or GY, and if you do, send the targeted monster to the GY. If this card is sent to the GY by card effect: You can send the top 5 cards of your Deck to the GY. You can only use each effect of "Tearlaments Kitkallos" once per turn.

justification: The Pendulum Effect of "D/D/D Deviser King Deus Machinex" allows for the Special Summon of a monster from the Pendulum Zone and the placement of another Pendulum Monster into a scale, clearly continuing and extending plays. Its Monster Effect's alternative Xyz Summon condition also facilitates easier summoning to extend combo lines, and its Quick Effect disruption provides protection for an already-established board, fitting the role of getting more value after a main combo has started.
card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
While you have a card in your other Pendulum Zone: You can target 1 Pendulum Monster you control or in your GY; Special Summon the card in your other Pendulum Zone, and if you do, place that targeted Pendulum Monster in your Pendulum Zone. You can only use this effect of "D/D/D Deviser King Deus Machinex" once per turn.

[ Monster Effect ] 
2 Level 10 Fiend monsters
You can also Xyz Summon this card by using a "D/D/D" monster you control as material. (Transfer its materials to this card.) You can only control 1 "D/D/D Deviser King Deus Machinex" in your Monster Zone. Once per Chain, when a Monster Card your opponent controls activates its effect (Quick Effect): You can either detach 2 materials from this card, or destroy 1 "Dark Contract" card you control, and if you do, attach that opponent's card to this card as material. Once per turn, during your Standby Phase: You can place this card in your Pendulum Zone.

justification: The card requires a specific monster to already be on the field ("Target 1 Level 3 or lower WATER monster you control") to activate, and its effect brings out another monster from the Deck. This serves to continue or extend existing plays by providing additional monster presence, even with the restriction on Extra Deck Special Summons.
card_text: Target 1 Level 3 or lower WATER monster you control; Special Summon 1 monster with the same name from your Deck, but you cannot Special Summon from the Extra Deck while you control that face-up Special Summoned monster.

justification: This card requires a "Number" monster to already be in the GY and a "Utopia" monster to be on the field for its main effect, meaning it cannot initiate a combo by itself. Instead, it utilizes these pre-existing resources to Special Summon and set up a powerful attack, extending plays and pushing for more value after a combo has begun.
card_text: When this card is Normal Summoned: You can Special Summon 1 non-LIGHT "Number" monster from your GY, but negate its effects, and if you do, equip both this card and 1 "Utopia" monster you control to it, each as an Equip Spell that gives it 1700 ATK, also you can only declare one attack for the rest of this turn. It requires you to control a card, fitting the "something to have started already" aspect of an Extender.

justification: This card significantly boosts an equipped monster's ATK, allowing it to overcome stronger opponent monsters or deal lethal damage. justification: Myutant Ultimus is a Fusion Monster activated from the Extra Deck, serving as an on-field omni-negate. It is also a desired card for the archetype's end-board, not an undesirable draw (not a Garnet). Therefore, it does not fit any of the defined categories.
card_text:
