card_text: Cannot be Special Summoned. Once per turn: You can target 1 other Spirit monster on the field; return it to the hand. Once per turn, during the End Phase, if this card was Normal Summoned or flipped face-up this turn: Return it to the hand.

justification: The monster effect requires another face-up card on the field to be destroyed alongside itself to trigger, indicating it extends existing plays rather than starting them. The Pendulum effect also relies on having "Supreme King Z-ARC" and another specific Pendulum card already on the field to activate.
card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
If you control "Supreme King Z-ARC", you take no damage. Once per turn, if you have "Supreme King Gate Infinity" in your other Pendulum Zone: You can destroy both cards in your Pendulum Zones, and if you do, add 1 "Polymerization" Spell Card or "Fusion" Spell Card from your Deck to your hand.

[ Monster Effect ] 
Once per turn: You can target 1 other face-up card you control; destroy both it and this card, and if you do, Special Summon 1 Dragon-Type Fusion or Synchro Monster from your Extra Deck, but its ATK and DEF become 0, it has its effects negated, also it cannot be used as a Material for a Synchro or Xyz Summon. If this card in the Monster Zone is destroyed by battle or card effect: You can place this card in your Pendulum Zone.

justification: This card's effect allows it to remove an opponent's monster from their field by equipping it. While it doesn't negate, it effectively neutralizes a monster's presence and can be used to break an opponent's established board, particularly as it can be used during your own turn.
card_text: Once per turn: You can discard 2 cards, then target 1 face-up monster your opponent controls; equip that face-up monster to this card. This card gains ATK equal to the combined original ATK of the monsters equipped to it by this effect. Once per turn, during the End Phase: You can target 1 Monster Card equipped to this card by this card's effect; Special Summon it to your field.

justification: This card's effects do not fit any of the defined categories. The Pendulum Effect is a continuous effect that negates other Pendulum Monsters, and the Monster Effect destroys itself and a battling Pendulum Monster. Neither is a starter, handtrap, extender, board breaker, or garnent.
card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
Negate the effects of face-up Pendulum Monsters while your opponent controls them.

[ Monster Effect ] 
At the start of the Damage Step, if this card battles a Pendulum Monster: Destroy both that monster and this card.

justification: This card can be Ritual Summoned and has an effect that allows it to special summon a Flip monster during the End Phase, which can begin a combo or establish a board presence on its own. Its additional effects to change monster positions can also facilitate future plays.
card_text:
You can Ritual Summon this card with "Prediction Ritual". Once per turn, during your End Phase: You can Special Summon 1 Flip monster from your hand or Graveyard in face-down Defense Position. You can only use 1 of the following effects of "Prediction Princess Tarotrei" per turn, and only once that turn.
● During either player's turn: You can target 1 face-down monster on the field; change it to face-up Attack Position.
● During either player's turn: You can target 1 face-up monster on the field; change it to face-down Defense Position.

justification: This card's effect is a conditional damage effect that triggers when specific "Red-Eyes" monsters are destroyed. It does not initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend a current play, or break an established board. It's primarily a payoff for having a board and having it destroyed.
card_text: If a face-up "Red-Eyes" monster(s) you control, except "Red-Eyes B. Chick", is destroyed by battle or card effect: Target 1 of those monsters; both players take damage equal to its original ATK. You can only activate 1 "Red-Eyes Burn" per turn.

justification: This card allows for the search of "Polymerization" from the Deck or GY, which is a key component for Fusion Summons. This ability to access a vital combo piece early in the game makes it a strong starter.
card_text: Once per turn: You can discard 1 card; add 1 "Polymerization" from your Deck or GY to your hand. Once per turn, during the End Phase: You can target 1 monster in your GY that was used as a Fusion Material for a Fusion Summon this turn; add it to your hand.

justification: This card's effect can only be activated when the player takes damage, implying that some prior action or interaction has already occurred. It then allows for further Special Summoning, extending the player's combo or field presence, but with a restriction on the Extra Deck, which is typical of extender effects.
card_text: If you take damage: You can Special Summon this card from your hand, also you cannot Special Summon monsters from the Extra Deck for the rest of this turn, except "Raidraptor" monsters.

justification: This card’s effect is activated from the hand during the opponent's turn to disrupt their plays by changing monster attributes and preventing effect activations, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.
card_text:
2+ Level 9 monsters
Once per turn (Quick Effect): You can detach 1 material from this card and declare 1 Attribute; this turn, all face-up monsters on the field become that Attribute, also all monsters in your opponent's possession with that Attribute cannot activate their effects or attack. Monsters that "True Draco" and "True King" monsters in your hand would destroy with their effects can be chosen from your opponent's field.

justification: This card allows you to reveal specific monsters from your Deck and add one to your hand, initiating your plays by fetching key combo pieces.
card_text: Reveal 3 Warrior-Type monsters from your Deck, including at least 1 "Black Luster Soldier" monster or "Gaia The Fierce Knight" monster, and have your opponent randomly pick 1. If they picked a "Black Luster Soldier" monster or "Gaia The Fierce Knight" monster, add it to your hand and send the rest to the Graveyard. Otherwise, send them all to the Graveyard. You can only activate 1 "Beginning of Heaven and Earth" per turn.

justification: This card can be activated from the hand to search for a key combo piece (a LIGHT Machine Union monster), allowing for meaningful progress in starting your deck's main strategy.
card_text: When this card is activated: You can add 1 LIGHT Machine Union monster from your Deck to your hand. Once per turn, if a LIGHT Machine Union monster(s) is Normal or Special Summoned to your field (except during the Damage Step): You can target 1 of those monsters; equip 1 appropriate LIGHT Machine Union monster, with a different name, from your Deck to that monster, but the Union monster you equipped cannot be Special Summoned this turn. You can only activate 1 "Union Hangar" per turn.

justification: This card's primary effect allows for Level manipulation, which is often used to facilitate Synchro or Xyz Summons after a prior play has been made. The second effect provides a search, but it requires the card to be Tributed, implying it's already been used in some capacity or is being used to extend a combo. It doesn't initiate plays on its own or disrupt the opponent from the hand.
card_text: Once per turn: You can reveal any number of "Nekroz" cards in your hand; increase or reduce this card's Level by the number of revealed cards until the end of this turn. If this card is Tributed by a card effect: You can add 1 "Nekroz" monster from your Deck to your hand, except a Ritual Monster. You can only use this effect of "Ariel, Priestess of the Nekroz" once per turn.

justification: This card's effect can be activated during the opponent's turn when they declare an attack, directly disrupting their offensive plays. While it doesn't negate, it offers a variety of disruptive outcomes that align with the strategic role of a handtrap.
card_text:
When an opponent's monster declares an attack: Roll a six-sided die and apply the result.
1: Halve your LP.
2: Make that attack a direct attack.
3: Choose 1 monster you control, change the attack target to it, and perform damage calculation.
4: Choose 1 other monster your opponent controls, change the attack target to it, and perform damage calculation.
5: Negate the attack, and if you do, inflict damage to your opponent equal to that monster's ATK.
6: Destroy that opponent's monster.

justification: The card has an effect that can be activated during either player's turn by banishing a "Kozmo" monster from the Graveyard to negate a Trap Card's activation, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.
card_text: Cannot be targeted by an opponent's card effects. During either player's turn, when a Trap Card is activated: You can banish 1 "Kozmo" monster from your Graveyard; negate the activation, and if you do, destroy that card. If this card is destroyed by battle or card effect and sent to the Graveyard: You can banish this card from your Graveyard; add 1 Level 8 or lower "Kozmo" monster from your Deck to your hand.

justification: This card's effect can be activated from the hand when no monsters are controlled, allowing it to Special Summon itself and generate resources (tokens), which is the primary requirement for a starter card to begin a combo.
card_text: Cannot be Normal Summoned/Set. Must first be Special Summoned (from your hand) while you control no monsters. When this card is Special Summoned from the hand: You can Special Summon 2 "Vague Shadow Tokens" (Winged Beast-Type/DARK/Level 1/ATK 0/DEF 0), but they cannot be Tributed or be used as Synchro Material. You can banish this card and 1 or more face-up non-Tuners you control, then target 1 "Blackwing" Synchro Monster in your Graveyard whose Level equals the total Levels the banished monsters had on the field; Special Summon it, and if you do, it is treated as a Tuner.

justification: This card's primary effect is to Special Summon a Union monster from the GY when Normal Summoned. This requires the player to already have a Union monster in the GY, indicating that another card or effect has already initiated the play. It then provides further utility by allowing itself to be equipped to or Special Summoned to extend plays.
card_text: If this card is Normal Summoned: You can target 1 Union monster in your GY; Special Summon it. Once per turn, you can either: Target 1 Machine monster you control; equip this card to that target, OR: Unequip this card and Special Summon it. Your opponent cannot target a monster equipped with this card with card effects, also if the equipped monster would be destroyed by battle or card effect, destroy this card instead.

justification: This card can be activated from the hand and allows for continued plays by manipulating "Subterror" monsters on the field or providing protection against negation, pushing combos forward after an initial play has started.
card_text:
Activate 1 of these effects, also, after that, Set this card face-down instead of sending it to the GY.
● Change 1 Set "Subterror" monster on the field to face-up Attack or Defense Position.
● Change 1 face-up "Subterror" monster on the field to face-down Defense Position.
● ATK/DEF of 1 "Subterror" monster on the field become equal to its combined original ATK/DEF until the end of this turn.
● This turn, activated effects of "Subterror" cards cannot be negated.

justification: This card can be activated from the hand by discarding another Rock monster, allowing it to Special Summon another Rock monster from the Deck, which can be the initial piece of a combo and doesn't require any prior setup.
card_text: You can discard 1 Rock monster; Special Summon from your Deck, 1 Rock monster that has the same original Attribute and Level as that monster, in face-up Attack Position or face-down Defense Position. You can only use this effect of "Doki Doki" once per turn.

justification: This card is a Fusion Monster that requires specific Fusion Material Monsters to be Summoned. Its effect is not specified, and it does not inherently initiate plays, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend existing plays, break boards, or serve as a desirable-but-bricky piece for another card's effect. Therefore, it doesn't fit any of the provided categories based solely on its summoning condition.
card_text: "Fusionist" + "Enchanting Mermaid"

justification: This card's effect allows it to destroy multiple of the opponent's monsters when it destroys one by battle, effectively clearing the opponent's established board, especially when going second. The ATK gain effect also helps it overcome strong monsters.
card_text:
2 Level 4 Winged Beast-Type monsters
If this card is Xyz Summoned, and your opponent's LP is at least 3000 higher than yours: You can make this card gain 3000 ATK. When this card destroys an opponent's monster by battle: You can detach any number of Xyz Materials from this card; destroy exactly that number of monsters your opponent controls.

justification: The card accumulates Spell Counters over time and can then use them to set a Quick-Play Spell from the GY, which extends the player's plays by providing additional resources or combo pieces. It does not inherently start a combo but rather builds upon existing play patterns involving Spell activations.
card_text: Each time a Spell Card is activated, place 1 Spell Counter on this card when that Spell resolves. This card's Level is increased by the number of Spell Counters on it. You can remove 3 Spell Counters from this card, then target 1 Quick-Play Spell in your GY; Set that card to your Spell & Trap Zone. You can only use this effect of "Magical Something" once per turn.

justification: This card's Pendulum Effect can prevent the opponent from activating Trap Cards, and its Monster Effect can lock the opponent out of the Extra Deck. These disruptive effects, combined with its ability to be Pendulum Summoned, allow it to be a strong opening card to establish control and hinder the opponent's plays.
card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
While you control an "Amorphage" monster, neither player can activate Trap Cards or their effects, except for "Amorphage" cards. Once per turn, during your Standby Phase, Tribute 1 monster or destroy this card.

[ Monster Effect ] 
If this card is Pendulum Summoned or flipped face-up, neither player can Special Summon monsters from the Extra Deck while this card is face-up on the field, except "Amorphage" monsters.

justification: This card's effect activates when an attack is declared, offering protection to monsters. It does not inherently start a combo, disrupt the opponent from the hand, extend existing plays, break boards, or act as a necessary but undesirable draw. It is a defensive utility card that doesn't fit the defined categories.
card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
Once per turn, when an attack is declared involving a monster you control and an opponent's monster: You can activate this effect; that monster you control cannot be destroyed by that battle.

[ Monster Effect ] 
Once per turn, when an attack is declared involving a monster you control and an opponent's monster: You can activate this effect; that monster you control cannot be destroyed by that battle.

justification: This card’s effect allows for reactive disruption (preventing targeting of SPYRAL monsters) and provides a search function, fitting the typical handtrap role of reacting to opponent’s plays while also offering a way to continue plays.
card_text: Your opponent cannot target other "SPYRAL" cards you control with card effects. Once per turn: You can add 1 "SPYRAL" monster from your Deck to your hand. Once per turn, during your End Phase, shuffle 1 monster from your Graveyard into the Deck or destroy this card.

justification: The card's ultimate effect allows the player to send it to the Graveyard to shuffle all of the opponent's cards on their hand, field, and Graveyard into the Deck. This is a powerful disruption that clears the opponent's established threats, making it a board breaker, especially when used to disrupt a developed board. The other effects are stat boosts or protection, which are supportive but not the primary role of this card as a game-ending or board-clearing effect.
card_text:
This card gains these effects depending on the number of face-up "Zefra" monsters with different names in your Extra Deck.
● 3 or more: Monsters you control gain 100 ATK for each face-up card in any Extra Deck.
● 5 or more: Monsters you control cannot be destroyed by an opponent's card effects.
● 8 or more: Monsters you control cannot be targeted by an opponent's card effects.
● 10: You can send this card to the Graveyard; shuffle all cards from your opponent's hand, field, and Graveyard into the Deck.

justification: This card allows for the Special Summon of an "Super Quantal Mech Beast" Xyz Monster from the Extra Deck by discarding a card and controlling a "Super Quantum" monster. This can initiate the deck's combo by establishing an Xyz Monster on the field, making it a strong opening play.
card_text: Discard 1 card, then target 1 "Super Quantum" monster you control; Special Summon from your Extra Deck, 1 "Super Quantal Mech Beast" Xyz Monster with the same Attribute as that monster you control by using it as material. (This is treated as an Xyz Summon.) You can send this card from the Field Zone to the GY, then target 3 "Super Quantal Mech Beast" Xyz Monsters with different names you control and/or in your GY; Special Summon 1 "Super Quantal Mech King Great Magnus" from your Extra Deck, and if you do, attach the targeted monsters and their materials to it as material.

justification: This card's effects are primarily reactive or provide passive benefits to "Dinomist" monsters. The Pendulum Effect requires being in the Pendulum Zone to negate a targeting effect, and the Monster Effect banishes monsters destroyed by battle, neither of which initiates a combo, disrupts the opponent from the hand, extends existing plays, or breaks a board.
card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
Once, while this card is in your Pendulum Zone, you can negate an activated card effect that targets another "Dinomist" card(s) you control, then destroy this card.

[ Monster Effect ] 
Banish any monster destroyed by battle with a "Dinomist" monster you control.

justification: This card's effect allows it to search for a Ritual Spell upon being Ritual Summoned, which is the first step in executing a Ritual Monster-based strategy. This makes it a key card to initiate plays.
card_text: You can Ritual Summon this card with "Machine Angel Ritual". If this card is Ritual Summoned: You can add 1 Ritual Spell from your Deck or GY to your hand. If this card is Tributed: You can make all Ritual Monsters you control gain 1000 ATK/DEF.

justification: The card's primary effect requires another "D/D" monster to be Special Summoned while it is on the field, enabling further plays and extending combos. It does not initiate a combo on its own.
card_text:
2 "D/D" monsters
If another "D/D" monster(s) is Special Summoned to your field, while you control this face-up card (except during the Damage Step): You can target 1 "D/D" monster in your Graveyard; Special Summon it. You can only use this effect of "D/D/D Flame King Genghis" once per turn. If this card is destroyed by battle, or if this card in its owner's possession is destroyed by an opponent's card effect: You can target 1 "Dark Contract" card in your Graveyard; add it to your hand.

justification: This card's effect requires specific monsters to be on the field to activate, and its primary function is to shuffle cards on the field into the deck and summon a monster. This allows it to disrupt an established opponent's board, fitting the role of a board breaker, especially when going second.
card_text: If a "Dracoslayer" monster, that is not a Pendulum Monster, and a "Dracoverlord" monster are on the field: Shuffle as many cards on the field as possible into the Deck, then you can Special Summon 1 "Dracoslayer" or "Dracoverlord" monster from your Deck, ignoring its Summoning conditions.

justification: This card is a Fusion Spell that provides buffs and additional attacks to a specific monster when equipped. It doesn't initiate plays by itself, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend existing combos, break boards, or function as a "necessary brick." Its effect is entirely dependent on other cards being on the field.
card_text: If you have 5 or more Dragon-Type Synchro Monsters with different names in your Graveyard: Special Summon 1 "Stardust Dragon" from your Extra Deck (this is treated as a Synchro Summon), but banish it during the End Phase. Equip it with this card. While equipped, it gains ATK equal to the combined ATK of all Dragon-Type Synchro Monsters in your Graveyard. Also while equipped, when it destroys an opponent's monster by battle: You can banish 1 Dragon-Type Synchro Monster from your Graveyard; it can attack an opponent's monster again in a row.

justification: This card requires existing monsters on the field to be Xyz Summoned and then uses its effect to revive another monster, extending existing plays rather than initiating them.
card_text:
2 or more Level 4 monsters
Once per turn, you can also Xyz Summon "Zoodiac Chakanine" by using 1 "Zoodiac" monster you control with a different name as Xyz Material. (If you used an Xyz Monster, any Xyz Materials attached to it also become Xyz Materials on this card.) This card gains ATK and DEF equal to the ATK and DEF of all "Zoodiac" monsters attached to it as Materials. Once per turn: You can detach 1 Xyz Material from this card, then target 1 "Zoodiac" monster in your Graveyard; Special Summon it, but for the rest of this turn, it has its effects negated, also it cannot be used as an Xyz Material for a Summon.

justification: This card can be activated from the hand during either player's turn to disrupt the opponent by changing the card name they declared. The second effect to destroy a card on the field during the End Phase also serves as disruption or removal, fitting the role of a handtrap.
card_text: During either player's turn, when your opponent activates a card or effect by declaring exactly 1 card name: You can send this card from your hand to the Graveyard; declare 1 other card name. The card name your opponent declared becomes the card name declared by this effect. Once per turn, during either player's turn: You can target 1 face-up card on the field; destroy it during the End Phase of the next turn.

justification: This card requires an equipped monster to already be on the field to provide its protection effect. Its secondary effect of Special Summoning "Phantasm Spiral Dragon" and equipping itself also depends on the equipped monster having battled, thus extending existing plays rather than initiating them.
card_text: Equip only to a Normal Monster. The first time the equipped monster would be destroyed by battle each turn, it is not destroyed. At the end of the Battle Phase, if the equipped monster battled: You can Special Summon 1 "Phantasm Spiral Dragon" from your hand, Deck, or Graveyard, and equip it with this card, then if they have any, your opponent discards 1 card. You can only use this effect of "Phantasm Spiral Wave" once per turn.

justification: This card can be activated from the hand and initiates plays by Special Summoning a "Lunalight" monster from the Graveyard, allowing for early combo development.
card_text: Target 1 "Lunalight" monster in your GY; Special Summon it. You can banish this card from your GY, then discard 1 card; add 1 "Lunalight" monster from your Deck to your hand.

justification: This card's effect to destroy all Special Summoned monsters the opponent controls, activated by detaching an Xyz Material, is a powerful way to clear an established opponent's board, making it a board breaker. While it can also attack directly, its primary disruptive potential lies in its board-clearing ability.
card_text:
3 Level 5 Winged Beast-Type monsters
This card can attack directly while it has Xyz Material. When this card inflicts battle damage to your opponent: You can target 1 monster your opponent controls; destroy it. Once per turn: You can detach 1 Xyz Material from this card; destroy as many Special Summoned monsters your opponent controls as possible, and if you do, inflict 500 damage to your opponent for each monster destroyed.

justification: This card's effect requires a specific monster ("Junk Warrior") to be on the field and only provides battle-related benefits during the turn it is activated. It does not initiate plays, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend combos, break boards, or function as a necessary but undesirable draw like a Garnet. Its impact is situational and tied to an existing board presence.
card_text:
Target 1 "Junk Warrior" you control; if it battles an opponent's monster this turn while you control it, apply these effects.
● Your opponent cannot activate cards or effects until the end of the Damage Step.
● If it attacks a Defense Position monster, inflict piercing battle damage to your opponent.
● Double any battle damage your opponent takes.
● It cannot be destroyed by battle.
● Destroy the opponent's monster that battled it at the end of the Damage Step.

justification: The card can be Special Summoned from the hand when no monsters are controlled, and its effect allows it to add a "Super Quant" card from the GY to the hand, providing a strong opening play that can initiate the deck's strategy.
card_text:
If you control no monsters, you can Special Summon this card (from your hand). You can only use each of the following effects of "Super Quantum Red Layer" once per turn.
● When this card is Normal or Special Summoned: You can target 1 "Super Quant" card in your GY; add it to your hand.
● If this card is sent to the GY: You can target 1 "Super Quant" monster in your GY, except "Super Quantum Red Layer"; Special Summon it, but it cannot activate its effects.

justification: The card can be activated from the Graveyard during either player's turn to protect a "Crystron" monster by banishing itself, and this effect can be used to extend plays by ensuring a combo piece remains on the field or to disrupt the opponent's plays without needing to be on the field initially. The first effect to special summon also requires a banished "Crystron" to target, implying a prior play has been made.
card_text: Target 1 of your banished "Crystron" monsters; Special Summon it, and if you do, if your opponent controls any face-up monsters, change their DEF to 0. During either player's turn, except the turn this card was sent to the Graveyard, when a card or effect is activated that targets a "Crystron" monster you control: You can banish this card from your Graveyard; negate that effect.

justification: This card’s effect requires an existing board (at least one "Windwitch" monster) to activate, extending the combos that have already been established. It doesn’t initiate a new play but builds upon an existing one.
card_text:
"Windwitch - Winter Bell" + 1 "Windwitch" monster
You can target 1 monster in either player's Graveyard; until the End Phase, this card's name becomes that target's original name, and replace this effect with that target's original effects. If this card in your possession is destroyed by your opponent's card (by battle or card effect) and sent to your Graveyard: You can target 1 "Windwitch - Winter Bell" and 1 Level 4 or lower "Windwitch" monster in your Graveyard; Special Summon them. You can only use each effect of "Windwitch - Crystal Bell" once per turn.

justification: This card is activated from the hand during the opponent's turn in response to a specific battle condition, disrupting the opponent's offensive play by changing the attack target and potentially inflicting damage, fitting the role of a handtrap.
card_text: When an opponent's monster declares a direct attack, if its ATK is greater than or equal to your LP: Special Summon this card in Attack Position as an Effect Monster (Warrior-Type/LIGHT/Level 4/ATK ?/DEF 0) that has ATK equal to your LP, then change the attack target to this card. (This card is also still a Trap Card.) If this card Summoned this way is destroyed by battle, after damage calculation: Inflict damage to your opponent equal to its ATK.

justification: This card's ability to change an opponent's monster to face-down Defense Position, especially when activated during either player's turn if it has a specific material, can disrupt the opponent's board and potentially enable further plays for the user by clearing a problematic monster, thus extending their advantage or control. It doesn't initiate a combo on its own but supports an ongoing strategy.
card_text:
2 Level 4 monsters
Cannot attack unless it has Xyz Material. Once per turn: You can detach 1 Xyz Material from this card, then target 1 other face-up monster on the field; change it to face-down Defense Position. This effect can be activated during either player's turn, if this card has "Super Quantum Green Layer" as Xyz Material. Once per turn: You can attach 1 "Super Quantum" monster from your hand or field to this card as an Xyz Material.

justification: This card can be activated during either player's turn by tributing itself to negate a Special Summon and destroy the summoned monster(s). This disruptive ability, usable from outside of the field, aligns with the strategic role of a handtrap.
card_text:
1 Tuner Synchro Monster + 1 or more non-Tuner Synchro Monsters
During either player's turn, if your opponent would Special Summon a monster(s): You can Tribute this card; negate the Summon, and if you do, destroy that monster(s). During the End Phase, if this effect was activated this turn (and was not negated): You can Special Summon this card from your Graveyard. If this card is destroyed by battle, or if this face-up card you control leaves the field because of an opponent's card effect: You can Special Summon 1 Level 8 or lower "Warrior" Synchro Monster from your Extra Deck. (This Special Summon is treated as a Synchro Summon.)

justification: The card's primary effect, changing a monster's Level, can extend combos by enabling easier Xyz or Synchro summons. The second effect, adding a monster from the GY to hand upon a tribute, also provides resources to continue plays after a board presence has been established. It doesn't necessarily start a combo on its own and is more about continuing or enhancing existing plays.
card_text: You can Ritual Summon this card with "Primal Cry". Once per turn: You can reveal 1 monster in your hand, then target 1 face-up monster on the field; that target's Level becomes equal to the Level the revealed monster had, until the end of this turn. Once per turn, if another monster is Tributed from your hand or field (except during the Damage Step): You can target 1 monster in your Graveyard; add it to your hand.

justification: This card's effects, particularly negating a monster's effects and reducing its ATK to 0, or destroying all monsters on the field when it's destroyed, are primarily used to dismantle an established opponent's board, making it a board breaker. While it has a revival effect, its primary utility in disrupting an opponent's board is more prominent.
card_text:
1 "Predaplant" monster + 1 DARK monster whose original Level is 8 or higher
Must first be Fusion Summoned. Once per turn: You can target 1 face-up monster on the field; until the end of this turn, change that target's ATK to 0, also that target has its effects negated. If this card is destroyed and sent to the Graveyard: Destroy as many monsters on the field as possible, then you can banish 1 Level 8 or higher DARK monster from your Graveyard and Special Summon this card from the Graveyard.

justification: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand when the player controls no monsters, allowing it to initiate plays by itself and begin a combo.
card_text: If you control no monsters, you can Special Summon this card (from your hand). If this card destroys an opponent's monster by battle: This card gains 500 ATK. If this card is destroyed by battle: The monster that destroyed it loses 500 ATK.

justification: The monster effect allows it to send a "D/D" or "Dark Contract" card from the Deck to the GY upon Normal or Special Summon, which is a strong opening play to set up subsequent combos.
card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ]

justification: This card's effect activates automatically during the Standby Phase and applies a random outcome that can benefit or harm the player, or disrupt the opponent. During each of your Standby Phases: Roll a six-sided die and apply the result.
● 1: Discard 1 card.
● 2: Your opponent discards 1 card.
● 3: Draw 1 card.
● 4: Your opponent draws 1 card.
● 5: Destroy 1 monster your opponent controls.
● 6: Destroy this card.
